name: Chocolate Rain Forest Praline Torte
recipeIngredient: 2 Large Egg Whites, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the Rain Forest Praline: Line a baking sheet with foil and butter lightly. Set aside.
- In a small, heavy saucepan, combine sugar and 1/4 cup water. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until sugar is dissolved. Increase heat to medium-high and bring syrup to a boil without stirring; cook until caramelizes and turns amber color. Remove pan from heat and stir in toasted nuts for about 1 minute. Pour mixture onto prepared baking sheet and let cool completely.
2. Make the Rain Forest Cocoa Meringue Round:
- Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a baking sheet with foil and lightly butter it, dusting with flour.
- In food processor fitted with metal blade, combine toasted Brazil nuts, cashews, and 4 tablespoons sugar. Process for 30-40 seconds until finely ground. Add flour and cocoa powder; pulse on and off 4 or 5 times until well blended.
- Whip egg whites until stiff peaks form. Gradually add remaining 2 tablespoons sugar; whip until shiny and stiff but not dry. Sprinkle nut mixture over beaten whites; fold in with a rubber spatula until just blended. Fill pastry bag fitted with medium plain tip (such as Ateco #5) with mixture.
- Pipe spiral pattern of meringue about 1/2-inch thick into outlined circle on baking sheet. If there are any holes, pipe meringue into spaces; using a small offset metal spatula, spread evenly.
- Bake rounds for about 25 minutes until edges begin to brown. Remove from oven and let cool completely. Peel off aluminum foil from bottom of meringue.
3. Make the Chocolate Curls:
- Temper chocolate according to directions. Spread tempered chocolate on clean, dry counter or work surface. Holding a metal dough scraper or spatula at 45-degree angle, push away from you; chocolate will curl upon itself. Using bamboo skewer, lift curls onto foil-lined baking sheet and set aside until ready for assembly.
4. Make the Caramel Rum Syrup:
- In small heavy saucepan, combine sugar and 1/4 cup water. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly with wooden spoon until sugar is dissolved. Increase heat to medium-high and bring syrup to a boil without stirring; cook until caramelizes and turns amber color. Remove pan from heat and gradually pour in remaining 1/4 cup water (mixture will bubble up). Return pan to heat and bring caramel to boil, stirring to dissolve any hardened caramel.
- Remove pan from heat and let cool. Stir in rum. Set aside until ready for assembly.
5. Make the Rainforest Praline Ganache:
- Place chocolate in 4 1/2-quart bowl of heavy-duty electric mixer. In small saucepan over medium heat, bring cream to gentle boil.
- Pour hot cream over chocolate; let stand 30 seconds to melt chocolate. Gently whisk until smooth. Cover ganache and chill for 1 hour until thickens to pudding-like consistency.
- Line a 9-inch springform pan with cardboard cake circle. Trim meringue circle to fit in pan and brush with caramel rum syrup.
- Add 2 tablespoons syrup to ganache; place bowl in mixer stand. Whip ganache at high speed for about 45 seconds until lightens in color and almost doubles in volume. Using a small offset spatula, spread ganache evenly over meringue. Place torte on wire rack set over baking sheet; refrigerate while preparing glaze.
6. Prepare the Glaze:
- In 2-quart liquid measuring cup, place chocolate. In small saucepan over medium heat, bring cream and corn syrup to gentle boil.
- Pour cream over chocolate; let stand 30 seconds to melt chocolate. Gently whisk until smooth. Stir in vanilla. Let glaze cool for 15 minutes.
- To serve, run knife around edge of torte to loosen from springform pan side. Remove side of pan and transfer torte to work area. Using metal spatula, smooth sides and top; pour glaze over torte covering completely. Refrigerate cake for 15 minutes until glaze is set.
Yield: 12 servings
Preparation Time: 3 hours plus baking, cooling, and chilling times
